paper.title: Spectroscopic and Dynamic Orbital Analyses of Metal-Poor and High Proper Motion Stars: I. HD\,8724 and HD\,195633

review.summary: This paper presents high-resolution spectroscopic analyses, kinematic calculations, and dynamical orbital analyses of two metal-poor high proper-motion stars (HD 8724 and HD 195633) to investigate their Galactic origins. The paper contains eight internal LaTeX tables covering observational metadata, model atmosphere parameters, literature parameter compilations, elemental abundances, abundance sensitivity tests, astrometric and spectroscopic data, Galactic space velocities and orbital parameters, and globular cluster encounter probabilities. External resources include the NIST and VALD atomic databases, the MOOG abundance analysis code, and the Baumgardt Milky Way globular cluster database.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Provides key characteristics of the ELODIE spectral observations for HD 8724 and HD 195633, including coordinates, spectral types, exposure times, signal-to-noise ratios, heliocentric radial velocities, and observation dates.

source_refs.caption: Equatorial coordinates, spectral types, and {\sc ELODIE} spectral data were provided for HD\,8724 and HD\,195633, respectively. The corrected {\sc ELODIE} spectra accounted for the reported heliocentric radial velocities, which were cross-checked by the Solar spectrum.
source_refs.label: table:1

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Presents the element abundances [X/Fe] calculated for HD 8724 and HD 195633, together with solar abundances from this study and from Asplund et al. (2009). Abundances in bold were calculated via spectrum synthesis.

source_refs.caption: The element abundances [$X$/Fe] calculated for stars HD\,8724 and HD\,195633 are shown. Simultaneously, the Solar abundances obtained in this study (TS) and those reported by \citet{asplund2009}[ASP] are provided. Abundances in bold are those calculated via spectrum synthesis method.
source_refs.label: table:abund

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Reports the sensitivity of derived abundances to uncertainties in the model atmosphere parameters (effective temperature, surface gravity, and microturbulence) for HD 8724 and HD 195633.

source_refs.caption: Sensitivity of the derived abundances to the uncertainties of $\Delta T_{\rm eff} = +$115 K, $\Delta \log g = +$0.32 cgs, and $\Delta \xi = \pm$0.5 km s$^{\rm -1}$ in the model atmosphere parameters of HD\,8724 for $T_{\rm eff} =$ 4700 K, $\log g$ = 1.65 cgs, and $\xi$ = 1.58 km s$^{\rm -1}$. The derived uncertainties of $\Delta T_{\rm eff} = +$205 K, $\Delta \log g = +$0.35 cgs, and $\Delta \xi = \pm$0.5 km s$^{\rm -1}$ in the model parameters of HD\,195633 for $T_{\rm eff} =$ 6100 K, $\log g$ = 3.95 cgs, and $\xi$ = 1.26 km s$^{\rm -1}$ were also reported. The uncertainties in bold are those calculated via spectrum synthesis method.
source_refs.label: table:abund_err

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Reports the calculated Galactic space velocity components (U, V, W), total space velocity (S), and derived Galactic orbital parameters (maximum height Z_max, apocenter R_a, pericenter R_p, and eccentricity e) for HD 8724 and HD 195633.

source_refs.caption: The calculated space velocity components, total space velocities and obtained Galactic orbital parameters for HD\,8724 and HD\,195633.
source_refs.label: table:7

columns.name: \multirow2*Star | \cline2-8
columns.meaning: Name of the star

columns.name: U_\rm LSR | \multicolumn4c|(km s^\rm -1)
columns.meaning: Galactic space velocity component towards the Galactic center (relative to the LSR)

columns.name: V_\rm LSR | (kpc)
columns.meaning: Galactic space velocity component in the direction of Galactic rotation (relative to the LSR)

columns.name: W_\rm LSR
columns.meaning: Galactic space velocity component towards the North Galactic Pole (relative to the LSR)

columns.name: S_\rm LSR
columns.meaning: Total space velocity relative to the LSR

columns.name: Z_\rm max
columns.meaning: Maximum distance from the Galactic plane reached by the orbit

columns.name: R_\rm a
columns.meaning: Apocenter distance (farthest distance from the Galactic center)

columns.name: R_\rm p
columns.meaning: Pericenter distance (closest distance to the Galactic center)

columns.name: e
columns.meaning: Orbital eccentricity

internal_tables.evidence: The calculated space velocity components, total space velocities and obtained Galactic orbital parameters for HD\,8724 and HD\,195633.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Lists data for the five most likely globular clusters supporting the GC escape scenario for HD 8724 and HD 195633, including spatial and velocity encounter probabilities, metallicities, ages, and magnesium abundances.

source_refs.caption: Data for the five most likely GCs that support the GC escape scenario for HD\,8724 and HD\,195633.
source_refs.label: tab:final

columns.name: Cluster
columns.meaning: Name of the globular cluster

columns.name: l | (^o)
columns.meaning: Galactic longitude

columns.name: b | (^o)
columns.meaning: Galactic latitude

columns.name: P(\rm \theta) | (%)
columns.meaning: Spatial encounter probability based on position difference

columns.name: P(\rm \nu) | (%)
columns.meaning: Velocity encounter probability based on velocity difference

columns.name: P(\rm origin | \theta, \nu) | (%)
columns.meaning: Combined probability of GC origin from spatial and velocity encounter

columns.name: [Fe/H] | (dex)
columns.meaning: Iron abundance of the cluster

columns.name: \tau | (Gyr)
columns.meaning: Age of the globular cluster

columns.name: [Mg/Fe] | (dex)
columns.meaning: Magnesium-to-iron abundance ratio of the cluster

columns.name: References
columns.meaning: Literature references for the cluster parameters

internal_tables.evidence: Data for the five most likely GCs that support the GC escape scenario for HD\,8724 and HD\,195633.
internal_tables.comments: The table is split into two blocks (HD 8724 candidates and HD 195633 candidates) with a separator row showing the stellar parameters of each program star.

external_resources.description: NIST Atomic Spectra Database, used as a source of atomic data (wavelengths, log gf, excitation energies) for line identification and equivalent width analysis.

source_refs.context: Atomic data were sourced from NIST\footnote{NIST Atomic Spectra Database http://physics.nist.gov/PhysRefData/ASD} and VALD\footnote{VALD Atomic Spectra Database http://vald.astro.uu.se} databases.

external_resources.description: MOOG stellar atmosphere line analysis code by Sneden (1973), used under LTE conditions for elemental abundance determinations.

source_refs.context: the line analysis code MOOG \citep{sneden1973}\footnote{Source code of the MOOG software is available at \href{http://www.as.utexas.edu/$\sim$ chris/moog.html}{http://www.as.utexas.edu/$\sim$ chris/moog.html}} operating under LTE conditions was used.

external_resources.evidence: the line analysis code MOOG \citep{sneden1973}\footnote{Source code of the MOOG software is available at \href{http://www.as.utexas.edu/$\sim$ chris/moog.html}{http://www.as.utexas.edu/$\sim$ chris/moog.html}} operating under LTE conditions was used.
external_resources.comments: The paper cites a URL with LaTeX syntax ($\sim$) that is not a valid concrete URL; left empty per fidelity rules.

external_resources.description: 4th version of the Milky Way Globular Cluster Database (Baumgardt & Hilker), providing tidal radii and escape velocities used in the GC encounter probability calculations.

source_refs.context: their values are taken from 4th version of the Milky Way GC Database\footnote{\href{https://people.smp.uq.edu.au/HolgerBaumgardt/globular/}{https://people.smp.uq.edu.au/HolgerBaumgardt/globular/}}.
